methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Airship Industries is a seed-stage company operating in the Drones & Autonomous sector, founded by James Coutre. The comparable universe for this company consists of 7 private firms in the same sector, including Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and Thoron. No public companies trade directly in this narrow subsector, limiting traditional public market benchmarking.

Peer-median valuation methodology proved inapplicable to this analysis. The Drones & Autonomous sector contains only 4 same-sector peers overall, and filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comps yielded fewer than the required 3 datapoints necessary for robust median triangulation. A broader sectoral median would introduce excessive noise and misrepresent the company's specific risk profile and capital efficiency relative to its true peer set.

Given data constraints, this valuation cannot be supported by triangulation across multiple methods at this time. A defensible valuation range for Airship Industries would require either (1) deeper diligence into the financial profiles of the 7 private-sector peers to establish stage-adjusted revenue or capital-efficiency multiples, or (2) delayed re-assessment once additional comparable transactions or disclosed metrics emerge in the autonomous systems space. Key uncertainties remain the company's revenue traction, capital efficiency relative to later-stage drone developers, and path to a defensible competitive moat within a capital-intensive sector.
